questions.question: What type of law focuses on issues regarding the promises that businesses make with their customers, vendors, and other business partners?
questions.explanation: Contract. Contract law is an area of law that governs oral and written agreements—promises—that involve the exchange of goods, services, money, and/or property. Constitutional law is a body of law that addresses issues related to the Constitution and Constitutional rights. Probate is the legal process of validating a will and distributing assets to beneficiaries. A patent protects an owner's invention from use and distribution by unauthorized individuals. [pp. 208-209]. Boston: McGraw-Hill/Irwin.
sources: SOURCE: McAdams, T., Neslund, N., & Neslund, K. (2007). Law, business, and society (8th ed.)

questions.question: Tasks in a channel of distribution must be completed

options.A: in a specific order, with no overlap.
options.D: no matter how many intermediaries there are.

questions.explanation: No matter how many intermediaries there are. Tasks in a channel of distribution (e.g., transporting products, promoting products, negotiating with customers) must be completed no matter how many intermediaries there are. This may or may not occur within 30 days. The tasks may be performed by manufacturers, retailers, wholesalers, or any other intermediaries within the channel. The timeline for completing these tasks may overlap—for instance, gathering marketing information and financing are both ongoing actions.
sources: SOURCE: CM LAP 2—Chart Your Channels (Channel Management)

questions.question: The Parnell Company buys a variety of similar goods and sells them to specialty shops for resale. What type of intermediary is the Parnell Company?
questions.explanation: Wholesaler. A wholesaler is an intermediary who helps to move goods between producers and retailers by buying goods from producers and selling them to retailers (e.g., specialty shops). The retailers then sell the goods to the end users or ultimate consumers. Agents are intermediaries who assist in the sale and/or promotion of goods and services but do not take title to them. An industrial user is a business that buys materials, services, or goods, which it uses to make other goods or uses for company operations.
sources: SOURCE: CM LAP 1—Channel It (Channels of Distribution)

questions.question: Which of the following is a benefit of using technology in channel management:

options.A: It decreases the accuracy of orders.
options.B: It eliminates the need for employees.

questions.explanation: It increases efficiency. Technology can be used to increase the efficiency of channel management. Although it can save a company time and money, it does not mean that no employees are needed. It increases the accuracy of orders and decreases delivery times. http://smallbusiness.chron.com/channel-management-techniques-3072.html
sources: SOURCE: Ingram, D. (2014). Channel management techniques. Retrieved November 24, 2014, from

questions.question: An electronics manufacturer requires wholesalers to purchase a line of computer printers to get the laptop computers that they actually want to purchase. What unethical business practice does this situation exemplify?
questions.explanation: Full-line forcing. A business is engaging in full-line forcing when it requires its intermediaries to carry an entire line of its products (e.g., computer printers) that they may not want, so the intermediaries can get the one item or a few of the items that they do want (e.g., laptop computers). Many businesses consider this practice unethical, and it is an illegal activity in many jurisdictions if it hinders competition. Exclusive dealing occurs when one business forbids another channel member from buying a competitor's products. Smoothing is a practice that involves offering consumers a discount for purchasing products that otherwise would go to waste. A gray market involves the buying and selling of brand goods through unauthorized channels. McGraw-Hill/Irwin.
sources: SOURCE: Etzel, M.J., Walker, B.J., & Stanton, W.J. (2007). Marketing (14th ed.) [pp. 401-403]. Boston:

questions.question: A company has an online store that distributes products directly to consumers, no matter where they are in the world. Which part of the marketing mix does channel management directly affect in this example?
questions.explanation: Place. Place is a marketing element focusing on considerations in getting a selected product in the right place at the right time. Channel management encompasses the processes by which marketers ensure that products are distributed to customers efficiently and effectively. Although channel management affects price, promotion, and product, it is not directly illustrated in this example. http://tutor2u.net/business/marketing/distribution_introduction.asp 2015 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM—KEY 15
sources: SOURCE: Riley, J. (2012). Distribution (Place)—Introduction. Retrieved November 25, 2014, from

questions.question: For the past several months, a wholesaler has been shipping orders to a particular retailer later than it does to other retailers that sell the same products. In this situation, what is likely to occur?
questions.explanation: Vertical conflict. Vertical conflict is a type of channel conflict that occurs between channel members at different levels within the same channel. When a wholesaler treats one retailer differently from others, the retailer is likely to experience conflict with the wholesaler. In the situation described, the wholesaler is not providing the retailer with products that it needs at a particular place and time. The situation described is not an example of upward competition, bilateral cooperation, or indirect collaboration. Western, Cengage Learning.
sources: SOURCE: Kurtz, D. (2013). Contemporary marketing: 2013 edition (pp. 117, 426). Mason, OH: South-

questions.question: Which of the following things should you be sure to do when following directions:

options.A: Multitask to accomplish more tasks at once.
options.B: Avoid asking questions about the directions.
options.C: Start immediately after reading the first step.
options.D: Read through all of the directions before beginning. 2015 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM 2

questions.explanation: Read through all of the directions before beginning. When following written directions, it's important to read through all of the directions before beginning. Starting immediately after reading the first step can lead to confusion or cause you to miss something. If you have questions, you should ask. It's important to focus on following the directions, and it can be hard to pay attention when multitasking. http://www.ehow.com/way_5292894_strategies-following-directions.html
sources: SOURCE: Beal, J. (1999-2014). Strategies for following directions. Retrieved October 8, 2014, from

questions.question: A coworker asks you, “What did I miss in yesterday's meeting?” What type of question is this?
questions.explanation: Open. Open questions typically begin with what, why, or how. They aren't answered with a simple “yes” or “no.” Open questions ask for someone's opinion, knowledge, or feelings. Closed questions are considered “yes or no” questions. It is not likely to be inappropriate for your coworker to ask about yesterday's meeting. Leading questions are usually closed questions that try to “lead” someone to one answer. September 24, 2014, from http://www.mindtools.com/pages/article/newTMC_88.htm
sources: SOURCE: Mind Tools. (1996-2014). Questioning techniques: Asking questions effectively. Retrieved

questions.question: Matt works in customer service and often has to deal with customer inquiries. Which of the following should he do if he wants to respond in an appropriate way:

options.A: Spend as much time talking to and answering the customer as possible.
options.B: Answer the question immediately, even if he doesn't know the answer for sure.
options.C: Act disinterested while the customer is asking a question.
options.D: Pay close attention to the question and ask for clarification if needed.

questions.explanation: Pay close attention to the question and ask for clarification if needed. When answering inquiries, it's important to pay close attention to the question. If Matt wants to offer great customer service, he should be sure to ask for clarification if necessary. He should not answer the question if he doesn't know the answer. He should show the customer that he is interested in his/her question. He shouldn't spend more time than necessary answering customer questions—it's important to be concise when answering inquiries. October 2, 2014, from http://www.celebritystaff.com/blog/2009/11/05/top-techniques-for- effectively-answering-questions-at-work/
sources: SOURCE: Celebrity Staff. (2009). Top techniques for effectively answering questions at work. Retrieved

questions.question: You have a big paper due for your marketing class, and you're almost finished! All you have left to do is check your grammar, punctuation, and spelling. Which step of the writing process do you have left to complete?
questions.explanation: Editing. During the editing stage of the writing process, you give your words and sentences a close look and correct any grammar, punctuation, and spelling mistakes. Revising comes before editing and involves making bigger changes, such as rearranging or removing whole sections of writing. http://www.dailywritingtips.com/the-writing-process/ 2015 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM—KEY 16
sources: SOURCE: Hale, A. (2014). The writing process. Retrieved September 24, 2014, from

questions.question: While Mark is presenting his status report during a staff meeting, Danielle has a question about something he says. What should Danielle do?

options.A: Interrupt Mark so she can ask her question right away
options.B: Wait until the next meeting to ask her question
options.C: Ask the question after Mark has finished speaking
options.D: Wait to see if another participant has the same question

questions.explanation: Ask the question after Mark has finished speaking. Speakers often ask other meeting participants if they have questions after they finish their presentations. Therefore, Danielle should ask her question after Mark finishes his presentation, when he asks if anyone has questions. Interrupting Mark while he is speaking to ask a question is disruptive and rude. Other meeting participants may not think of the same question that Danielle has, so she should not rely on others to ask the question. Danielle should not wait until the next meeting because the information Mark provides may be outdated or forgotten by that point. Upper Saddle River, NJ: Pearson Prentice Hall.
sources: SOURCE: Bovée, C.L., & Thill, J.V. (2008). Business communication today (9th ed.) [pp. 47-48, 53].

questions.question: If a customer deals with an employee who is rude and unhelpful, the customer will likely

options.A: recommend the store to his/her friends.
options.B: make a large purchase from the company.
options.C: believe that the company has a rude and unhelpful image.
options.D: understand that the employee doesn't represent the store.

questions.explanation: Believe that the company has a rude and unhelpful image. Employees are the faces of their companies. Customers usually see an employee's behavior as an extension of the company's image. If that employee is rude and unhelpful, then the customer will see the entire company as being rude and unhelpful. The customer sees the employee as a representative of the store. If an employee is rude and unhelpful, a customer is unlikely to make a large purchase or recommend the store to his/her friends. October 2, 2014, from http://work.chron.com/importance-employee-behavior-organization- 11019.html
sources: SOURCE: McQuerrey, L. (2014). Importance of employee behavior in an organization. Retrieved on

questions.question: Today, customer relationship management is considered an important tool in
questions.explanation: Business decision making. In the past, customer relationship management (CRM) was used mainly as a way to track and organize information about customers so a business could identify its most valuable clients and tailor its marketing strategies to generate more revenue. Today, its uses have expanded, and it is also considered an important tool in business decision making. CRM is not typically considered a tool for use in commercial investing, hiring new employees, or establishing connections with vendors. These are not customer-focused activities.
sources: SOURCE: CR LAP 16—Know When to Hold 'Em (Nature of Customer Relationship Management)

questions.question: Rashid's business always puts the customer first. All of the company's decisions are made to satisfy customer wants and needs. Rashid's company is using the __________ concept.
questions.explanation: Marketing. The marketing concept is a philosophy of conducting business that is based on the belief that all business activities should be aimed toward satisfying consumer wants and needs while achieving company goals. While utility and the marketing concept go hand-in-hand, the utility concept is not a term commonly used to refer to the marketing concept. The marketing concept is also not typically referred to as the customer concept or the relative concept.

questions.question: Which of the following is an advantage of private enterprise:

options.A: The equal distribution of property and income
options.C: The freedom to use private property to make a profit
options.D: The elimination of poverty 2015 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM 3

questions.explanation: The freedom to use private property to make a profit. Individuals and businesses in a private enterprise economy are free to decide how to use their private property and when and how to sell it. The periods of unemployment that occur in a private enterprise are a disadvantage, not an advantage. In a private enterprise system, there is an unequal distribution of property and income, and poverty is not eliminated. 2015 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM—KEY 17
sources: SOURCE: EC LAP 15—People Power (The Private Enterprise System)

questions.question: A company just purchased a new warehouse. Which type of taxes will it need to pay on this warehouse?
questions.explanation: Property tax. Property tax includes tax spent on real estate. Because the company owns real estate (the warehouse), it must pay property tax. Estate tax is collected on inheritances. Excise tax is collected on specific commodities like tobacco, gambling, gasoline, etc. The term “building tax” is not commonly used to refer to property tax. 2014, from http://smallbusiness.chron.com/six-types-taxes-people-businesses-must-pay- 18773.html
sources: SOURCE: Uhlig, D.K. (2014). Six types of taxes people and businesses may pay. Retrieved October 3,

questions.question: Which of the following businesses suffers from a lack of standardization:

options.A: A company makes its products a different way every time.
options.B: A manager doesn't adequately explain company goals to employees.
options.C: Business employees spend their time arguing with each other.
options.D: An employee's outdated computer makes it difficult to work.

questions.explanation: A company makes its products a different way every time. A business that makes its products differently each time that it produces them suffers from a lack of standardization. If tasks and processes are not done the same way every time, then employees cannot be as productive as they could be if the company standardized its production processes. A manager who doesn't adequately explain company goals to employees is harming productivity due to a lack of communication. Coworkers who spend their time arguing with each other are dealing with personality conflicts. A lack of tools or equipment can harm productivity because workers can't do their jobs if they don't have ready access to all the tools they need—in this case, an up-to-date computer.
sources: SOURCE: EC LAP 18—Make the Most of It (Productivity)

questions.question: Haley is a ski instructor, but her job at the resort only runs from November through March. Now, it's April, and she's looking for another job. This is an example of __________ unemployment.
questions.explanation: Seasonal. Haley's situation is an example of seasonal unemployment. Some jobs are more abundant at certain times of the year. This can create unemployment for workers in the “off-season.” Frictional unemployment is voluntary unemployment that results from normal life circumstances. Technological unemployment happens when technological advances eliminate certain job positions. Cyclical unemployment rises and falls with ups and downs in the business cycle.
sources: SOURCE: EC LAP 29—Help Wanted? (Impact of Unemployment Rates)

questions.question: Patricia's department is receiving a new coworker. Patricia has heard that this coworker is quite a bit older than her, and she and her current coworkers are afraid that the coworker will be out of touch with current technology and difficult to work with. What action should Patricia take to deal with this situation?

options.A: Acknowledge that her beliefs are based on stereotypes.
options.B: Talk to her boss about hiring someone else.
options.C: Try to teach the new coworker how to use technology.
options.D: Avoid the new coworker and take over her duties when possible.

questions.explanation: Acknowledge that her beliefs are based on stereotypes. Stereotypes are set images or assumptions about a person or thing. If Patricia assumes that her new coworker will be out of touch with current technology and difficult to work with because she is older, she believes a stereotype. Patricia is assuming something about a person without getting to know her personally. Until Patricia acknowledges that her beliefs are based on stereotypes, she will not be able to change. She should not talk to her boss about hiring someone else, try to teach the new coworker how to use technology, or avoid the new coworker and take over her duties. All of those actions involve continuing to believe in her stereotypical assumptions. from http://www.beyondprejudice.com/reduce_your.html
sources: SOURCE: Beyond Prejudice. (n.d.). Reducing your prejudicial behavior. Retrieved October 8, 2014,

questions.question: Which of the following is true of self-confidence:

options.B: It cannot be attained unless you are born with it.
options.C: It often offends or annoys other people.
options.D: It inspires confidence in friends and coworkers.

questions.explanation: It inspires confidence in friends and coworkers. People with self-confidence inspire confidence in others, including their coworkers and friends. When you are confident, others believe in you. Although it can take time and effort to cultivate, anyone is capable of building his/her self-confidence. Self-confidence on its own does not offend or annoy people, and it should not involve bragging. If someone is annoying or offending others with his/her self-confidence, s/he may be overconfident. http://www.mindtools.com/selfconf.html 2015 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM—KEY 18
sources: SOURCE: Mind Tools. (1996-2014). Building self-confidence. Retrieved October 3, 2014, from

questions.question: Which of the following is a primary dimension of diversity:
questions.explanation: Age. Primary dimensions of diversity are those inborn differences that cannot be changed and affect a person's entire life. They include such characteristics as age, gender, race/ethnicity, nationality, physical traits, and abilities (both mental and physical). Language, family status, and income level are all considered secondary dimensions of diversity.
sources: SOURCE: EI LAP 11—Getting to Know You (Cultural Sensitivity)

questions.question: Josie has been working late nights and weekend hours at her job. She's losing sleep, getting sick, and feeling unexcited about her work. Josie is likely suffering from
questions.explanation: Burnout. Burnout is a type of exhaustion that is usually caused by too much stress. If you're exhausted from being stressed out too long, you might end up dealing with burnout. Conflict is a disagreement. Flextime is a scheduling procedure in which employees select their own working hours as long as they work the required number of hours. Productivity is the amount of work employees perform in a given period, usually their output per hour.
sources: SOURCE: EI LAP 25—Keep Your Cool (Stress Management)

questions.question: Lara does well at work because she is driven to accomplish her goals and meet a high standard of success. She likely has a high

options.D: achievement orientation. 2015 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM 4

questions.explanation: Achievement orientation. Achievement orientation is a drive to accomplish one's goals and to meet or exceed a high standard of success. Achievement-oriented people often want to do things better or more efficiently than they have been done in the past. The status quo is the way things have always been done. The employee turnover rate is the rate at which a business gains and loses employees. If Lara has a high achievement orientation, she is unlikely to have a high rate of failure.
sources: SOURCE: EI LAP 10—High Hopes (Developing an Achievement Orientation)

questions.question: To build positive political coalitions in the workplace, employees need to
questions.explanation: Cultivate support. Political coalitions often form in the workplace when two or more employees work together in an attempt to influence company policies and implement change. To effectively influence company policies, the employees must cultivate or gain support from those who hold power within the organization. To cultivate support, employees should be assertive rather than aggressive. Being indifferent or acting uncaring will not generate positive action or change in the workplace. Thomson South-Western.
sources: SOURCE: DuBrin, A.J. (2007). Fundamentals of organizational behavior (4th ed.) [p. 263]. Mason, OH:

questions.question: Monica is shopping for a new car. Car A costs $15,000, and Car B costs $17,000. Monica can deduce that Car B is more valuable by using money as a(n)
questions.explanation: Unit of measure. Money is used as a unit of measure. This is an important function of money because it allows people to determine the worth of products. Functions of money also include being a store of value and a medium of exchange, but those functions are not demonstrated in this example. This is not an example of money being used as an incentive. 2014, from http://www.cliffsnotes.com/test-prep/ap-tests/ap-economics-what-are-the- functions-of-money
sources: SOURCE: CliffsNotes. (2014). AP economics: What are the functions of money? Retrieved October 7,

questions.question: Margot wants to be sure that when she dies, her money, house, and cars will go to her sister. Margot should create a
questions.explanation: Will. A will is a legal document that outlines how a person wishes his/her assets to be distributed once s/he dies. If Margot wants to be sure that her sister receives her assets, she should set up a will. Retirement funds, college savings funds, and bank accounts do not guarantee that Margot's assets will be given to her sister. http://www.usa.gov/topics/money/personal-finance/wills.shtml 2015 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM—KEY 19
sources: SOURCE: U.S. Government. (2014). Writing a will. Retrieved October 7, 2014, from

questions.question: Your annual income, an inheritance, and the money from the sale of an asset are all considered events that contribute towards your
questions.explanation: Tax liability. A tax liability is tax that a person or business must pay the government. Certain events, like annual income, an inheritance, and money from the sale of an asset, are taxable. Since citizens must pay money on these sources of income, it is not untaxable income or a tax loophole. Sales tax is a type of tax that is paid on the sale of goods and services. http://www.investopedia.com/terms/t/taxliability.asp
sources: SOURCE: Tax liability. (2014). Retrieved October 7, 2014, from

questions.question: A business wants to start selling its products in foreign markets. Managers study financial information to determine if the company has the resources to undertake this new venture. This is an example of using financial information to
questions.explanation: Plan business expansion. Moving into foreign markets is a type of business expansion, and managers must ensure the company can afford it. Moving into foreign markets is not a way to reduce expenses, monitor ongoing business operations, or check up on the competition.
sources: SOURCE: FI LAP 9—By the Numbers (The Need for Financial Information)

questions.question: When a company brings in money, it affects the balance sheet by
questions.explanation: Increasing assets. When a company brings in money, it will likely be recorded on the balance sheet under “assets” and will increase the business's overall assets. It should not increase liabilities. It may decrease liabilities, but probably won't eliminate them. Incoming money should not decrease owners' equity.
sources: SOURCE: FI LAP 10—The Right Balance (The Nature of Balance Sheets)

questions.question: A company that sells securities to the public must

options.B: guarantee that securities will always be valuable.
options.D: tell investors about the risks of these securities.

questions.explanation: Tell investors about the risks of these securities. Companies that sell securities have to tell the truth about the securities they sell. They must be honest about their business and the risks of securities. Securities cannot be guaranteed because they always involve some degree of risk. Investors should ask questions and do research on securities; a company should not discourage them from doing so. 9, 2014, from http://investor.gov/introduction-markets/role-sec#.VDa8xPldXZJ
sources: SOURCE: U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. (2013). The role of the SEC. Retrieved October

questions.question: Which of the following phrases is most likely to be listed in an official job description:

options.D: “Possess an associate's degree or higher” 2015 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM 5

questions.explanation: “Edit company newsletter.” Editing the company newsletter is a task that would be listed in an official job description. A job description is an explanation of the specific responsibilities associated with a certain job. A job specification, on the other hand, is an explanation of the skills, knowledge, and characteristics required for a job. Having 10-15 years of experience, being able to work closely with others, and possessing an associate's degree or higher are all examples of what might be listed in a job specification.
sources: SOURCE: HR LAP 35—People Pusher (Nature of Human Resources Management)

questions.question: Marketers for a new brand of soda must decide which stores will sell their product. To help with this decision, the marketers analyze data for their previous products to see how well they sold in various stores. The marketers are using data to develop __________ strategies.
questions.explanation: Place. Getting products into customers' hands is a key task that marketers face. They must decide how much of the product to buy or create and what channels of distribution to use. Data about customers can provide insights into where those customers like to shop for certain products. For example, the marketers in this example can analyze data for their previous products to see how well they sold in various stores. This can help the marketers make a decision about where to sell their new brand of soda. This is not an example of using data to develop promotional, pricing, or product strategies. 2015 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM—KEY 20
sources: SOURCE: IM LAP 12—Data Do It (Need for Marketing Data)

questions.question: A food manufacturer is considering introducing a new line of breakfast cereal, so it sets up a focus group to find out if potential customers are interested. The company is collecting __________ information.
questions.explanation: Primary. Primary information is developed from data that are collected for use in one particular situation. These primary data are gathered in a systematic manner to solve a problem, to explore an opportunity, or for any other specific purpose useful to a business. Primary data can be gathered through questionnaires, focus groups, personal interviews, and test markets. Secondary information comes from data that have already been collected for other purposes. Internal data are found inside a business. This is not an example of sales information.
sources: SOURCE: IM LAP 2—Get the Facts Straight (Marketing-Information Management)

questions.question: Hailee receives a survey in the mail. At first, she thinks the survey is just market research to find out her cleaning product preferences. However, as she nears the end of the survey, she realizes it is actually attempting to sell her a specific brand of cleaning product. The survey comes from a company engaging in
questions.explanation: SUGGING. Selling under the guise of research (SUGGING) occurs when organizations administer surveys to gain customers or make sales, not to conduct market research. This practice is generally frowned upon because it is deceptive and it may make consumers skeptical of actual market research. Fundraising under the guise of research is known as FRUGGING and occurs when an organization tries to obtain donations by claiming to collect market research. CAN-SPAM is a U.S. law that sets rules and establishes standards for commercial email. This is not an example of self-regulation. Retrieved November 12, 2014, from http://www.dmnews.com/sugging-and-frugging- practices-as-ugly-as-they-sound/article/94425/
sources: SOURCE: Blumberg, M. (2007). SUGGING and FRUGGING: Practices as ugly as they sound.

questions.question: A shoe company wants to keep up with changing trends in the shoe industry, so it conducts marketing research. The company wants to be sure its marketing research is as effective as possible, so the researchers try to keep their own opinions and biases hidden so that their research will be
questions.explanation: Objective. Researchers must keep their own opinions out of the way when they are gathering data. If a researcher's biases are obvious to research respondents, then the respondents may give answers that they think the researcher wants to hear, rather than what they actually believe or know to be true. If marketing research is to be effective, researchers must be as objective (not subjective) as possible. Although marketing research does need to be timely, this isn't illustrated in this example. Effective marketing research is valid research.
sources: SOURCE: IM LAP 5—Seek and Find (Marketing Research)

questions.question: Businesses must be careful when using external sources to obtain marketing-research information because the methods used to collect the data may be
questions.explanation: Unreliable. Marketers often use external sources (e.g., independent research agencies and news articles) to obtain information for marketing-research purposes. External sources provide marketers with secondary information, which is data collected for purposes other than the issue at hand. Because the business may not know exactly how the data were collected or analyzed, the business may not know if or how many errors occurred during the research process. If errors occurred during the research process, the information may be unreliable. Marketing researchers may collect secondary information in inflexible, intuitive, or unintentional ways, but it must be reliable to be useful to the business.
sources: SOURCE: IM LAP 15—What's the Source? (Obtaining Marketing-Research Data)

questions.question: A marketing researcher wants to know which of the company's new products should be introduced to the market first. This is an example of a __________-oriented decision problem.
questions.explanation: Strategy. Decision problems can be strategy-oriented, or aimed at answering the questions “how?” or “which?” Discovery-oriented decision problems are aimed at answering questions like “what?” or “why?” Demographic and variable are not types of decision problems. 2015 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM—KEY 21
sources: SOURCE: IM LAP 13—What's the Problem? (Marketing Research Problems)

questions.question: A national fast food chain test markets its new sandwich in a few cities to gather data about its sales potential. Which type of marketing research is this?

options.D: Statistical 2015 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM 6

questions.explanation: Causal. Marketing research that focuses on cause and effect and tests “what if?” theories is called causal research. Causal research is typically completed by conducting experiments or simulations, which must be carefully designed and tightly controlled. This type of research is often very expensive. Examples of causal research include test marketing, allowing customers to shop in a simulated store and recording their actions, and conducting a clinical trial for a new medication. Exploratory research collects information to help the business define its issue, situation, or concern and decide the direction in which to go to address it. Descriptive research (also referred to as statistical research) gathers specific information related to an identified issue, situation, or concern.
sources: SOURCE: IM LAP 14—Better By Design (Marketing-Research Designs)

questions.question: Which of the following is a type of non-probability sampling design:
questions.explanation: Judgment sampling. Judgment sampling, also known as expert sampling, requires researchers to “hand- pick” respondents based on their knowledge or expertise. This is a type of non-probability sampling because it is not random. Cluster sampling, stratified random sampling, and systematic random sampling are all types of probability sampling.
sources: SOURCE: IM LAP 16—Take Your Pick (Nature of Sampling Plans)

questions.question: A clothing retailer wants to find out how it is performing against its competitors. It should use its sales data to perform a __________ analysis.
questions.explanation: Market share. Market share analysis allows a company to measure its sales performance against those of its competitors. Sales volume analysis allows a company to measure its actual sales against a number of different criteria. Demographic and primary data are not types of analyses that can be derived from sales data.
sources: SOURCE: IM LAP 11—Data Diving (Identifying Marketing Data)

questions.question: Which of the following is an advantage of the observation method of data collection:
questions.explanation: It reduces researcher bias. One advantage of the observation method of data collection is that it reduces researcher bias. Since the researchers aren't directly involved with the respondents, it is less likely that they will skew the data with their own opinions. The observation method raises concerns about privacy, is not fast, and does not explain motives and attitudes—these are all disadvantages of this method.
sources: SOURCE: IM LAP 17—Hunting and Gathering (Data-Collection Methods)

questions.question: Which of the following is an element of an effective survey:
questions.explanation: An explanation of its purpose. It's important to let survey participants know why a survey is being administered. This means explaining what information is being collected and how it will be used. Questions should be unbiased and as clear as possible. Instructions should be as short and concise as possible. http://www.sciencebuddies.org/science-fair-projects/project_ideas/Soc_survey.shtml 2015 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM—KEY 22
sources: SOURCE: Science Buddies. (2002-2014). Designing a survey. Retrieved October 4, 2014, from

questions.question: To process surveys containing open-ended questions, marketing researchers should first

options.A: discard the surveys that contain incorrect short-answer answers.
options.B: assign numerical values to the question options.
options.C: record the respondents' information in a spreadsheet.
options.D: develop categories in which to place the responses.

questions.explanation: Develop categories in which to place the responses. Because open-ended questions require more than a “yes” or “no” answer, the surveys containing these types of questions are qualitative in nature. This means that the survey participants' responses are based on their opinions, experiences, or expertise. For example, if a survey asks why respondents do not purchase products from a business, it is likely that respondents will provide a variety of unrelated answers. Therefore, marketing researchers develop categories in which to place the various responses. These categories might include the lack of product selection, high prices, poor service, inconvenience, etc. After developing categories, the marketing researchers can assign numerical values to the responses, record information on a spreadsheet, and determine how to handle surveys with non-response errors. The marketing researcher would not discard surveys that contain short-answer (open-ended) responses because these questions do not have incorrect answers. [p. 405]. Mason, OH: South-Western Cengage Learning.
sources: SOURCE: Churchill, G.A., Brown, T.J., & Suter, T.A. (2010). Basic marketing research (7th ed.)

questions.question: Interviewers cause marketing-research errors when they

options.A: ask the respondents to answer all of the questions.
options.C: express a biased attitude when asking questions.
options.D: allow respondents to answer questions honestly.

questions.explanation: Express a biased attitude when asking questions. If an interviewer uses a particular tone of voice or emotionally charged words or expresses personal or biased opinions during an interview, respondents may feel uncomfortable and less willing to provide honest answers. When respondents do not answer interview questions honestly, the integrity of the marketing research is compromised. To reduce risks associated with improper interviewing techniques, interviewers should receive proper training, which helps them learn how to ask questions in an objective manner. Communicating clearly, ensuring that respondents answer all of the questions, and allowing respondents to answer questions honestly are ways in which interviewers can reduce the risk of marketing-research errors. [pp. 382-383, 390]. Mason, OH: South-Western Cengage Learning.
sources: SOURCE: Churchill, G.A., Brown, T.J., & Suter, T.A. (2010). Basic marketing research (7th ed.)

questions.question: An effective questionnaire should

options.A: be at least two pages long.
options.B: avoid jargon and use clear language.
options.D: present many topics in each question.

questions.explanation: Avoid jargon and use clear language. If your questionnaire uses technical terms or jargon, you run the risk of respondents not understanding your questions. Concise questions and clear language make it easier for everyone to understand what you're asking. Your questionnaire should be brief, not longer than two pages. Questions should be phrased objectively, not subjectively. Each question should present only one topic. 2014, from http://www.allbusiness.com/marketing/market-research/1959-1.html
sources: SOURCE: AllBusiness. (1999-2014). Effective questions for market research. Retrieved November 12,

questions.question: A company needs to conduct marketing research before launching a new product, but its marketing department doesn't have a lot of money. If it wants to keep costs down, it should
questions.explanation: Conduct online surveys. Some methods of marketing research are more expensive than others, and it's important for marketers to take their budgets into account when selecting a method. Online surveys are often a less expensive option than in-person interviews. A week-long focus group would likely be expensive. The company should not avoid doing marketing research entirely. 2014, from http://www.ehow.com/how_2067463_pick-market-research-methodology.html
sources: SOURCE: Dontigney, M. (2014). How to pick a market research methodology. Retrieved November 24,

questions.question: Monica wants to be a good employee and help her company achieve its goals. Which of the following actions should she take:

options.A: Make sure not to be more than half an hour late each day.
options.B: Come to work even when she's sick or has a family emergency.
options.C: Plan her vacation time wisely to make sure she isn't absent at crucial times.
options.D: Make up for missing many days by working even harder when she's at work. 2015 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM 7

questions.explanation: Plan her vacation time wisely to make sure she isn't absent at crucial times. It's hard to be a good employee if you're not at work. Monica should be sure to plan her vacation time wisely so she isn't gone at crucial times for her work team. It's important to be at work, but coming in when she's sick or has an emergency isn't always a good idea for Monica. Monica shouldn't be late; even ten minutes a day can add up quickly. Even if Monica works hard, it's still important for her to be present at work when she's scheduled to be there. 2015 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM—KEY 23
sources: SOURCE: MK LAP 2—Act Now! (Employee Actions and Company Goals)

questions.question: To obtain more of the market share, Morrison Snack Foods begins to sell its potato chips and crackers to Quickster Convenience Stores in Seattle, Washington, and its surrounding communities. This is an example of a company implementing a marketing ___________ in relation to the marketing-mix element of ____________.
questions.explanation: Tactic; place. A goal is an objective or desired outcome. A strategy is a plan of action for achieving a goal. A tactic is a specific activity that a business implements to carry out a strategy. Place is the element of the marketing mix that involves getting the selected product in the right place at the right time. Morrison is selling snack foods to Quickster, which is a specific action that makes the products accessible to and convenient for the customer. Therefore, the company is using a tactic to achieve goals related to the place element of the marketing mix. Price is the marketing element that requires marketers to determine the amount of money that they will ask for in exchange for their products. Promotion is the marketing element that marketers use to inform, persuade, or remind customers of their products. Product is the good, service, or idea that the business offers its customers.
sources: SOURCE: MP LAP 2—Pick the Mix (Nature of Marketing Strategies)

questions.question: In which section of the marketing plan is the following statement mostly likely to found: “The RTR Company will use customer-satisfaction surveys and will analyze a variety of reports and metrics to continuously evaluate its progress.”
questions.explanation: Performance and implementation. The performance and implementation section of a marketing plan details expected results and performance measurements. A SWOT analysis is a marketing tool that investigates a business's strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, and threats in an organized fashion. The marketing strategies and programs section of the marketing plan covers the business's plan for communicating with its customers. The executive summary typically appears at the beginning of the document and serves as an overview of the marketing plan.
sources: SOURCE: MP LAP 1—A Winning Plan (Nature of Marketing Plans)

questions.question: While William conducts a situation analysis, he researches how his company is performing in relation to similar businesses in the marketplace. What is William doing?

options.B: He is reviewing customers' sales records.
options.C: He is identifying a new market segment.
options.D: He is monitoring the external environment.

questions.explanation: He is monitoring the external environment. Environmental scanning is the component of a situational analysis that involves gathering information that will help marketers analyze and understand changes in the environment. Environmental scanning helps marketers identify trends so they can adapt to environmental changes. Because William is gathering information about competitors, he is monitoring external factors. William is not reviewing customers' sales records or identifying a new market segment. Analyzing economic trends involves reviewing information about interest rates, consumer spending, unemployment rates, etc. New York: McGraw-Hill Irwin.
sources: SOURCE: Katz, J. & Green, R. (2011). Entrepreneurial small business (3rd ed.) [pp. 31-32, 388-389].

questions.question: Which of the following is an advantage of qualitative sales forecasting:

options.B: It is ideal when a lot of data are available.
options.D: It lacks a high level of accuracy.

questions.explanation: It is relatively inexpensive. Qualitative sales forecasting is advantageous because it's relatively inexpensive compared to other types of forecasting. It's ideal in situations where hard facts or numerical data are difficult to find (e.g., a new business launch). Qualitative sales forecasting contains bias, so it's not objective or neutral. Because of this, it lacks a high level of accuracy, which is a disadvantage.
sources: SOURCE: MP LAP 5—Futurecast (The Nature of Sales Forecasts)

questions.question: “Helsinki is the capital of Finland” is an example of what type of information?
questions.explanation: Factual. Factual information is proven, true, and objective. The capital of a country is a fact. You can find factual information in sources like dictionaries and atlases. Criticism is an analysis of something or someone that identifies any shortcomings. Opinion is a person's point of view or belief about a topic. It is proven that Helsinki is the capital of Finland. c.edu/library/Documents/infochar.pdf 2015 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM—KEY 24
sources: SOURCE: Factual vs. analytical. (n.d.). Retrieved October 3, 2014, from http://www.tri-

questions.question: Which of the following is an ethical dilemma related to an employee providing information in the workplace:

options.A: Steven advises a customer that Simpson Electronics does not provide personal information to other companies without the customer's consent.
options.B: A customer asks Kate to mail a complete product catalog and a comprehensive price list to his/her business.
options.C: Melinda and two of her coworkers talk about their plans to work overtime next weekend.
options.D: Bob's new employer asks him to share confidential information about product-development processes that his previous employer uses.

questions.explanation: Bob's new employer asks him to share confidential information about product-development processes that his previous employer uses. In many situations, employers require employees to sign a nondisclosure agreement, which is a promise to maintain the confidentiality of the business's trade secrets. Bob's new employer is asking him to break the promise that he made to his previous employer and share the information, which presents an ethical dilemma for Bob. Mailing a product catalog and price list to a customer, discussing work plans with coworkers, and providing a customer with accurate information about a company's privacy policy are ethical actions. York: Glencoe/McGraw-Hill.
sources: SOURCE: Kimbrell, G., & Vineyard, B.S. (2006). Succeeding in the world of work (pp. 184-187). New

questions.question: Microsoft Windows, Linux, and Mac OS X are examples of

options.D: operating systems. 2015 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM 8

questions.explanation: Operating systems. Operating systems organize and control the hardware and software in a computer. All computers have operating systems, which include Microsoft Windows, Linux, and Mac OS X. These are not examples of computers, Internet providers, or anti-virus software. from http://computer.howstuffworks.com/operating-system.htm
sources: SOURCE: Franklin, C., & Coustan, D. (n.d.). How operating systems work. Retrieved October 7, 2014,

questions.question: Which of the following computer functions enables a computer user to bring the text and the graphics from documents and spreadsheets created in other software programs into a presentation software program:
questions.explanation: Import. The import function enables the computer user to copy or download graphics from other locations (e.g., other software programs and websites) to slides in a presentation software program. The drawing function enables the computer user to draw illustrations in a desktop publishing program. The style function enables computer users to format documents in a particular way. The caption function enables computer users to add text or a numerical label to identify or explain a graphic within a document. 15, 2014, from http://www.exec-comms.com/blog/2010/09/26/how-to-import-images-into- powerpoint/
sources: SOURCE: Griffin, I. (2010, September 26). How to import images into PowerPoint. Retrieved October

questions.question: Why might a company decide to use computer-aided design (CAD)?

options.A: It is a fast way of manufacturing the product.
options.B: It is cheaper than producing a physical prototype.
options.D: It increases time spent designing products.

questions.explanation: It is cheaper than producing a physical prototype. Computer-aided design (CAD) uses computer technology to design and modify products. Designers use CAD because it saves them the money and time of creating a prototype. CAD does not manufacture products; computer-aided manufacturing (CAM) does. CAD does not control or order stock. It decreases, not increases, the time spent designing products. October 6, 2014, from http://business-blog.ashbournecollege.co.uk/unit-2-managing-a- business/operations-management-2/using-technology-in-operations/
sources: SOURCE: Ashbourne College's Business Studies Blog. (n.d.) Using technology in operations. Retrieved

questions.question: Jasmine owns a boutique on a busy street. Although her shop is small, it gets a lot of foot traffic, and she has many customers. Which of the following security procedures should Jasmine follow:

options.A: Have another person present when she opens up in the morning.
options.B: Keep a large amount of cash in her cash register at all times.
options.C: Avoid making eye contact with customers who walk into the store.
options.D: Keep the lighting dim to create an upscale mood.

questions.explanation: Have another person present when she opens up in the morning. It's a smart idea for Jasmine to have another person present when she opens up for business in the morning. Two people are better able to check for security breaches. Jasmine should not keep a large amount of cash in the cash register. She should make eye contact with customers who walk into the store; this lets customers know that she recognizes them. The lighting should be bright so that she can see all customers clearly. from http://www.police.ufl.edu/community-services/robbery-prevention-for-businesses/ 2015 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM—KEY 25
sources: SOURCE: University of Florida. (2012). Robbery prevention for businesses. Retrieved October 6, 2014,

questions.question: Which of the following is a type of purchasing:
questions.explanation: Buying for resale. When a business purchases items for resale, it buys things to sell to other businesses or consumers. This is a type of purchasing. Strategic planning is one way that purchasing specialists can help their companies. Negotiation is one of the things purchasing specialists often do when selecting suppliers. Buying for departments is not a type of purchasing.

questions.question: A histogram shows

options.A: whether or not a product meets customer needs.
options.B: if a process is working or not.
options.C: which problem should be addressed first.

questions.explanation: The distribution of data. A histogram, or bar graph, shows the distribution of data. It is a simple but useful tool for quality management. Statistical process control shows if a process is working or not. A Pareto chart shows which problem should be addressed first. A house of quality matrix shows whether or not a product meets customer needs.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a fixed operating expense:
questions.explanation: Insurance. Insurance is a fixed operating cost—it does not increase or decrease with changes in production. Sales commissions, advertising, and travel expenses are all variable—they fluctuate with changes in production.
sources: SOURCE: OP LAP 9—Watch Your (Over) Head (Overhead/Operating Costs)

questions.question: When a company offers something better than its competitors, it has a __________ advantage.
questions.explanation: Competitive. A competitive advantage is the edge achieved by businesses that offer something better than their competitors. Positioning innovation is a type of innovation that occurs when the purpose of a product is changed. Creative destruction is a term coined by Austrian economist Joseph Schumpeter to describe how new goods and services can hurt existing goods and services. Demographics are the physical and social characteristics of the population.
sources: SOURCE: PD LAP 18—Ideas in Action (Innovation Skills)

questions.question: Patrick is having trouble working with one of his coworkers on a company project. He isn't sure what's wrong, but he knows that his coworker isn't contributing quality work to the project. If Patrick wants to solve this problem, what is the first step he should take?

options.D: Involve another coworker 2015 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM 9

questions.explanation: Define the problem. When trying to solve a problem, it's important to be sure you know what the problem truly is. For example, Patrick's coworker isn't contributing quality work to their project, but that is actually a symptom of the problem. The true problem could be that Patrick isn't clearly communicating the project's goals, that the coworker is overwhelmed, that the coworker needs more training, etc. Patrick cannot fix his problem until he clearly defines it. Patrick should not ignore the problem or involve another coworker. http://www.mindtools.com/pages/article/newTMC_00.htm
sources: SOURCE: Mind Tools. (1996-2014). What is problem solving? Retrieved October 6, 2014, from

questions.question: Ina is starting a new job, and she wants to be sure she makes a good first impression. Her new employer is likely to expect her to

options.B: show up no more than 20 minutes late.
options.C: do her work without asking questions.

questions.explanation: Be respectful to all coworkers. Employers expect their employees to treat everyone—supervisors, coworkers, and customers—with respect. Ina should show up on time, not up to 20 minutes late. If she has questions about her work, she should ask. Employers expect their employees to have soft skills. http://aawdc.org/youth-works/index.php/Job-Search-Tools/Employer-Expectations.html 2015 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM—KEY 26
sources: SOURCE: Youthworks! (2011). Employer expectations. Retrieved October 3, 2014, from

questions.question: Edward is a college student earning his business degree. In the evenings and on weekends, he bags groceries at a local store to earn money for school. “Grocery bagger” is Edward's
questions.explanation: Job. A job is a temporary step that is part of building a career. A career is a long-term journey that typically requires planning and growing. Edward is majoring in business, so his career will likely be in the business field. In the short term, however, he is working at a grocery store to make money; this is a job. Because Edward is learning about business, we can assume that bagging groceries is not his goal or his long-term plan. October 6, 2014, from http://www.ehow.com/info_8226168_difference-between-job- occupation-career.html
sources: SOURCE: Lawrence, G. (2014). The difference between a job, occupation, and career. Retrieved

questions.question: Inez runs an organic soap-making business. If she gives her soap a high price, she is most likely to attract customers who

options.A: like to buy from local merchants.

questions.explanation: Are looking for prestige. Different companies seek to attract different types of customers. Low prices attract customers who want to save money, while high prices attract customers looking for prestige and high quality. Higher prices are not necessarily going to attract customers who like to buy from local merchants or those who do their shopping online.
sources: SOURCE: PI LAP 2—The Price Is Right (Nature of Pricing)

questions.question: A large hardware store sells tools for very low prices-even lower than the price that the store paid for the tools! The store isn't making a profit right now, but it hopes to put a small, local hardware store out of business so that it will be the only hardware store in town. The large hardware store is trying to eliminate its competition by using
questions.explanation: Predatory pricing. Predatory pricing occurs when a company sets very low prices with the intent to drive the competition out of the market. Price gouging occurs when products are sold at unreasonably high prices; this often happens after a natural disaster, when supplies are scarce but needed. Price fixing is an illegal business agreement in which businesses agree on prices of their goods or services, resulting in little choice for the consumer. Prestige pricing is deliberately putting an artificially high price on a good or service to suggest high quality and status. http://www.investopedia.com/terms/p/predatory-pricing.asp
sources: SOURCE: Investopedia. (2014). Predatory pricing. Retrieved November 11, 2014, from

questions.question: You work for an online retailer that is having trouble with its pricing. It's difficult to stay up to date on your competitors' pricing, so you often find that your company isn't pricing competitively. It's hard to adjust prices manually when your store offers sales, and it's also confusing to figure out how to provide discounts to customers who order a large quantity of your products. Your store should consider

options.D: not trying to beat the competition.

questions.explanation: Utilizing product pricing software. Technology is able to impact the pricing function in the form of software. Pricing software can help businesses change and set prices easily. Some forms of pricing software can also monitor the prices of the competition to ensure that you're never being undercut. There is no reason for your store to discontinue online sales. 21, 2014, from http://www.distributionsoftware.co/blog/articles/a-flexible-pricing-strategy-can- impact-your-bottom-line/
sources: SOURCE: NAV-X. (2012). A flexible pricing strategy can impact your bottom line. Retrieved November

questions.question: What type of service do banks and insurance companies provide?
questions.explanation: Pure. There are two different types of services a business may perform. Services that are not attached to any tangible good are referred to as pure services. Banks and insurance companies provide pure services. Product-related services are attached to some sort of tangible good. Positioning and prototype are not types of services.
sources: SOURCE: PM LAP 17—Rapping Up Products (Nature of Product/Service Management)

questions.question: The goal of penetration pricing is

options.D: achieving rapid market development. 2015 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM 10

questions.explanation: Achieving rapid market development. The goal of penetration pricing (setting prices low) is to achieve rapid market development during the introduction stage of the product life cycle. At this point, the product is not creating a profit yet. Liquidating assets is something marketers might do in the decline stage. Recovering introduction costs is the goal of a price-skimming strategy (setting prices high). 2015 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM—KEY 27
sources: SOURCE: PM LAP 18—Get a Life (Cycle) (Impact of Product Life Cycles)

questions.question: Even though a company has the technology to produce curling irons and hair straighteners that last for years, it instead manufactures hair styling tools that break after about a year so that customers must frequently replace their products. This is known as
questions.explanation: Planned obsolescence. Planned obsolescence occurs when companies decide to make their products outdated quickly so that consumers will be forced to buy new products. Many consumers feel that planned obsolescence is unethical. This is not considered product labeling, product risk, or planned risk. http://www.investopedia.com/terms/p/planned_obsolescence.asp
sources: SOURCE: Investopedia. (2014). Planned obsolescence. Retrieved November 10, 2014, from

questions.question: Brian's company is trying to generate ideas for new products, so he volunteers to run the brainstorming meeting. He asks questions, listens to people's ideas, and writes down what they say on a whiteboard. Brian is the group's
questions.explanation: Facilitator. A facilitator is a person who guides a group's discussion and helps it adhere to its agenda. In Brian's case, he might help his idea-generation group by listening to people's ideas and asking them questions. He is not a producer or a generator—everyone in the group is involved in generating ideas, not just the facilitator. It is not necessary for a facilitator to be a manager. from http://scottberkun.com/essays/34-how-to-run-a-brainstorming-meeting/
sources: SOURCE: Berkun, S. (2004). #34—How to run a brainstorming meeting. Retrieved November 24, 2014,

questions.question: When a manufacturing company decides how many parts should be in each box, what the quality of those parts should be, and how quickly employees should make the parts and package them, it is setting product
questions.explanation: Standards. Standards are specifications or statements that are used as a basis for comparing or judging goods or services. The most common types of product standards include quantity standards, quality standards, time standards, and cost standards. Grades are ratings assigned to products that tell to what extent standards were met. The company is not setting sales standards. Costs are just one common type of product standard.
sources: SOURCE: PM LAP 8—Raise the Bar (Grades and Standards)

questions.question: The Do-Not-Call Implementation Act aims to protect consumers from
questions.explanation: Telemarketers. The Do-Not-Call Implementation Act aims to protect consumers from receiving calls from telemarketers by allowing them to sign up for the National Do-Not-Call Registry. It does not include provisions covering door-to-door solicitors, retail salespeople, or cell-phone companies.
sources: SOURCE: PM LAP 7—Protect and Serve (Consumer Protection)

questions.question: A shoe company has seven footwear lines. These seven different lines embody the company's product
questions.explanation: Width. Width, also called breadth, refers to the number of product lines a company carries. It is usually referred to as being narrow or broad. Length refers to the total number of products in the product mix. Depth refers to the assortment of sizes, colors, flavors, and models offered in a company's product lines. Consistency refers to how closely a company's product lines are related.
sources: SOURCE: PM LAP 3—Mix & Match (Nature of the Product Mix)

questions.question: Aurora is able to save money by buying her Internet and cable together instead of buying them separately. Aurora benefits from product
questions.explanation: Bundling. Product bundling is a marketing strategy that involves offering several products for sale as one combined product. This typically involves a discount for the customer. This is not called product grouping or seeding. 2014, from http://www.knowthis.com/setting-price-part-2/promotional-pricing-product- bundling
sources: SOURCE: KnowThis.com. (2014). Promotional pricing: Product bundling. Retrieved November 10,

questions.question: Marketers position a certain type of watch on the fact that it can run for years without needing to be replaced or repaired. This is positioning by
questions.explanation: Durability. Positioning a watch on the fact that it can run for years without needing to be replaced or repaired is positioning by durability. A product's durability does not relate to its ease of purchase or quick delivery. Competence is a quality that may be used in positioning by people (service). 2015 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM—KEY 28
sources: SOURCE: PM LAP 19—Getting Piece of Mind (Factors Used to Position Products/Services)

questions.question: A bike company envisions itself as young, active, and rugged. This is its brand

options.D: promise. 2015 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM 11

questions.explanation: Personality. Brand personality is the projection of a brand that encompasses its values and emotional connections with consumers. Brand identity is the elements that are instantly recognized as representing a particular business or product. Brand values are beliefs or qualities that a corporate brand stands for and is built around. A brand promise is a business's spoken or unspoken agreement with customers that it will consistently meet their expectations and deliver on its brand characteristics and values.
sources: SOURCE: PM LAP 20—Corporate Identity (The Nature of Corporate Branding)

questions.question: The target market's needs and expectations are factors that a business considers when
questions.explanation: Positioning its corporate brand. A corporate brand is the combined impressions and experiences associated with a particular business. Positioning involves using different tactics and strategies to create certain images or impressions in the minds of consumers. When positioning its corporate brand, the business considers the target market, which is the particular group of customers that the business seeks to attract. To attract a target market, the business must identify and provide what it wants and needs. If a business fails to respond to the target market's needs and wants, it is likely to have poor sales, which negatively affects the business's ability to compete in the marketplace. Although a business can take steps to accommodate product demand, it cannot control it. Hiring procedures and business records are not actions that identify the target market's specific needs and expectations. River, NJ: Prentice Hall.
sources: SOURCE: Kotler, P., & Lane, K. (2006). Marketing management (12th ed.) [pp. 314-316]. Upper Saddle

questions.question: Which of the following is a benefit of promotion:
questions.explanation: Increased customer loyalty. A benefit of promotional activities is often increased customer loyalty to certain products or businesses. Businesses spend billions of dollars on promotion to create specific company images. Customers identify with these images and see themselves in the roles or lifestyles shown in the promotions. Promotion typically costs the company money, but this is not one of its benefits. Promotion leads to increased sales and increased employment opportunities.
sources: SOURCE: PR LAP 2—Razzle Dazzle (Nature of Promotion)

questions.question: A company runs an anti-drug announcement on a local television station as a way of showing that it cares about the community. Which type of institutional promotion is this company using?
questions.explanation: Public service. Public-service promotions inform customers about noncontroversial issues that are in the public's best interest. Public-service promotions build goodwill for the company, especially when they depict the company as a friend of the community. Patronage promotions are designed to promote a firm's prestige or its features. Institutional promotion is not referred to as accidental or individual.
sources: SOURCE: PR LAP 4—Know Your Options (Types of Promotion)

questions.question: Angela is a sales rep who spends most of her time interacting with her customers. She even visits her potential customers in their offices to discuss her products and give sales presentations. Angela is engaging in
questions.explanation: Personal selling. Personal selling is a form of promotion that determines client needs and wants and responds through planned, personalized communication that influences purchase decisions and enhances future business opportunities. Publicity is an identity-building tactic defined as any nonpersonal presentation of ideas, goods, or services that is not paid for by the company or individual that benefits from or is harmed by it. Advertising is any paid form of nonpersonal presentation of ideas, images, goods, or services. Sales promotion is promotional activities other than advertising, personal selling, and publicity that stimulate customer purchases. 2015 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM—KEY 29
sources: SOURCE: PR LAP 1—Spread the Word (Nature of the Promotional Mix)

questions.question: Which of the following is an advantage of using the Internet as a promotional tool for your business:

options.A: It is more cost effective than traditional advertising.
options.B: It can make it easier for hackers to find information.
options.C: Tracking results is difficult and slow.
options.D: It reaches fewer people than traditional promotion.

questions.explanation: It is more cost effective than traditional advertising. Using the Internet to promote your business is often less expensive than buying traditional advertisements in newspapers, magazines, or on television or radio. It also makes tracking your results quick; it's easier to see how customers are responding to an online ad or a social media profile than it is to track how they're responding to a television commercial. Internet promotions can reach a different group of customers, but it does not necessarily reach fewer people (and if it did, that would not be an advantage). Hackers finding business information is not an advantage. November 21, 2014, from http://www.huffingtonpost.com/women-co/business-marketing- advice-_b_1555615.html
sources: SOURCE: The Huffington Post. (2012). Promote your business: 6 ways technology can help. Retrieved

questions.question: The primary reason that governments regulate the ways in which businesses market goods and services to children is to protect the children's
questions.explanation: Safety and privacy. Children have the right to privacy and safety. Although today's youth are very technology-oriented, many lack an understanding of the safety issues associated with Internet use. Problems often occur when young Internet surfers access inappropriate websites and unknowingly provide personal information. Privacy breaches, identity theft, cyber stalking, etc., may occur when inappropriate information is given out. Therefore, governments often develop laws to protect young Internet users. Businesses that market goods and services to children must be familiar with these regulations so they can design and implement their online activities legally. The government may penalize and fine businesses that violate these regulations. Young children do not have obligations and cannot obtain credit. resource guide for parents. Retrieved October 16, 2014, from https://www.privacyrights.org/childrens-online-privacy-a-resource-guide-for-parents
sources: SOURCE: Privacy Rights Clearinghouse. (2014, April). Fact sheet 21: Children's online privacy: A

questions.question: Which of the following is a type of direct mail:
questions.explanation: Email advertising. Direct mail is a promotional medium that comes to consumers' homes and businesses in the form of letters, catalogs, postcards, folders, faxes, and emails. Billboards are a form of out-of-home media. Television commercials and radio advertising are forms of broadcast media.
sources: SOURCE: PR LAP 3—Ad-quipping Your Business (Types of Advertising Media)

questions.question: Mark doesn't have much money to promote his new restaurant, so he focuses on inviting a few customers in for a special dinner in hopes that they will post favorable reviews online and tell their family and friends about their great meals. Mark is using __________ marketing.

options.D: cause 2015 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM 12

questions.explanation: Grassroots. Grassroots marketing involves relying on a group of customers to spread a company's marketing message. Cause marketing occurs when a company partners with a non-profit/charity in a way that benefits both organizations. Mark is not using direct mail or broadcast marketing. http://smallbusiness.chron.com/definition-grassroots-marketing-23210.html
sources: SOURCE: Myers, C. (2014). Definition of grassroots marketing. Retrieved November 7, 2014, from

questions.question: Which of the following is an advantage of using the Internet for direct marketing:

options.A: It involves face-to-face contact with customers.
options.B: It eliminates the need for other types of marketing.
options.C: It is less flexible than other forms of marketing.
options.D: It is a relatively affordable form of marketing.

questions.explanation: It is a relatively affordable form of marketing. Internet marketing is a popular direct marketing choice because it can be more affordable than other direct marketing methods. It is also more flexible, more targeted, and more responsive than other direct marketing choices. It does not necessarily eliminate the need for other types of marketing. Direct marketing via the Internet does not involve face-to-face contact with customers, which is one of the reasons why it is so affordable. http://www.mplans.com/articles/direct-marketing-fundamentals/ 2015 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM—KEY 30
sources: SOURCE: Berry,T. (n.d.) Direct marketing fundamentals. Retrieved November 7, 2014, from

questions.question: A company released a video game that depicts the game's characters using a certain brand of equipment. What type of sales promotion is the business using?
questions.explanation: Advergaming. This sales-promotion tactic involves placing products in video games to generate brand awareness or to reinforce brand loyalty. For example, a racing video game might allow the players to select a specific car brand or model (e.g., Ford or Chevrolet) to “drive” or race against other virtual cars. In another video game, the characters might use a specific type of athletic equipment or wear a certain brand of clothing. Promoting specific brands through video games is not an example of cooperative selling, bloggertainment, or bonus packaging. (5th ed.) [p. 428]. Upper Saddle River, NJ: Pearson Prentice Hall.
sources: SOURCE: Soloman, M.R., Marshall, G.W., & Stuart, E.W. (2008). Marketing: Real people, real choices

questions.question: A food manufacturer just released a new product. The company's public relations department writes an article about the health benefits of the product and includes recipes and photos. A cooking magazine prints the article in its pages for a cost. What PR tool is this company using?
questions.explanation: A matte release. Matte releases are articles PR teams generate for placement in print or online publications. A food manufacturer might submit an article that features recipes involving their product. Podcasts are digital audio files that can be used for public relations activities. Crisis management is the process PR departments use to deal with a sudden negative emergency. A matte release of this type would likely not be considered philanthropy. November 20, 2014, from http://nextcommunications.blogspot.com/2010/10/matte-releases- pr-branded-content-for.html
sources: SOURCE: Escovedo, R. (October 2010). Matte releases: PR branded content for the newbie. Retrieved

questions.question: White space is used in advertisements because it

options.C: makes advertisements less costly to produce.

questions.explanation: Increases readability. White space is often used in advertisements because it makes words easier to read. White space makes advertisements look less busy. White space doesn't necessarily have to be white; it's simply space between pictures and words in advertisements. It can be any color. White space is used to make advertisements more effective, not to save time for designers. 2014, from http://www.serif.com/blog/how-important-is-white-space-in-print-design/
sources: SOURCE: Mauloni, M. (2014). How important is white space in print design? Retrieved November 7,

questions.question: An important activity performed by a business's public-relations staff is
questions.explanation: Crisis management. Crisis management is a public-relations strategy that involves the ongoing managing, planning, and coordinating of resources to handle undesirable situations or circumstances. Market segmentation is the process of dividing a total market into smaller, specific groups. Territory development refers to the expansion of a selling area. Talent acquisition is the process of finding and hiring employees. communications (4th ed.) [pp. 364-367]. Upper Saddle River, NJ: Prentice Hall.
sources: SOURCE: Clow, K.E., & Baack, D. (2010). Integrated advertising, promotion, and marketing

questions.question: Which of the following is an internal audience for public-relations activities:
questions.explanation: Employees. Public relations specialists must deal with both internal and external audiences. Employees, those people within the company, are an internal audience. Groups outside of the company, like customers, the media, and the community, are external audiences. 2014, from http://www.slideshare.net/sslyngdoh/internal-and-external-publics-of-public- relations-by-shaining-star-lyngdoh
sources: SOURCE: Lyngdoh, S. (2014). Internal and external publics of public relations. Retrieved November 21,

questions.question: Tierra owned the only coffee shop in town until a new coffee shop opened up a few blocks away. Now she tries to offer even better products at lower prices to make sure customers come to her shop instead of the new one. What role of selling in our economy does this illustrate?
questions.explanation: Promotes competition. Two coffee shops striving to provide products for the lowest prices and best values in town are competing with each other. This illustrates how selling promotes competition in our economy. Selling also affects employment, adds utility, and helps customers determine needs, but these are not illustrated in this situation. 2015 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM—KEY 31
sources: SOURCE: SE LAP 117—Sell Away (The Nature and Scope of Selling)

questions.question: Which of the following is true of soliciting feedback:

options.A: It isn't important unless customers complain.
options.B: It only needs to be done once.
options.C: It's best determined by a lengthy survey.
options.D: It should be convenient for the customer. 2015 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM 13

questions.explanation: It should be convenient for the customer. There's only one way to know if your customer is satisfied— ask! It's important to give your customers convenient, user-friendly ways to provide feedback. A short comment card or an online survey will probably be effective. It's best to avoid lengthy surveys, as they will just annoy your customers. Customer satisfaction is dynamic, so it's important to solicit feedback on a regular basis.
